User Story: As a user, I can open the editor app
Expected Behavior: Editor reaches ready state; loading veil disappears; no console errors

User Story: As an author, I can enter Play from the toolbar
Expected Behavior: Play opens the current world in play mode and hides editor-only controls

User Story: As a player, I can return to the editor
Expected Behavior: Editor button is visible in play mode and returns to authoring

User Story: As a player, I can move and sprint
Expected Behavior: WASD/arrows move relative to camera and Shift increases speed while remaining terrain-grounded

User Story: As a player, I can jump
Expected Behavior: Space initiates a grounded jump and the player returns safely to terrain/support

User Story: As a player, I can look and switch camera mode
Expected Behavior: Pointer lock controls look; Escape releases it; V switches first/third person; crosshair reflects first-person lock

User Story: As a developer, I can inspect runtime statistics
Expected Behavior: Backquote toggles debug HUD and B toggles the performance budget HUD

User Story: As an author, I can create and validate a world
Expected Behavior: World documents normalize supported settings and reject invalid data without corrupting runtime state

User Story: As an author, I can save and load my world
Expected Behavior: Objects and supported world settings round-trip through localStorage

User Story: As an author, I can import and export world files
Expected Behavior: A valid world document exports and imports with validation and identity preserved

User Story: As an author, I can export a playable build
Expected Behavior: World pack and playable ZIP include a validated manifest report and runnable world content

User Story: As an author, I can select objects from the hierarchy
Expected Behavior: Hierarchy lists world objects and selection stays synchronized with viewport and inspector

User Story: As an author, I can place primitive assets
Expected Behavior: Choosing an asset then clicking terrain creates a grounded selectable object

User Story: As an author, I can move rotate and scale selections
Expected Behavior: Gizmo and numeric inspector apply finite transforms and commit world/collider updates

User Story: As an author, I can edit multiple objects together
Expected Behavior: Toggle selection creates a group pivot and transforms every selected object consistently

User Story: As an author, I can duplicate and delete objects
Expected Behavior: Duplicate creates new identities; delete removes selected objects and their runtime effects

User Story: As an author, I can undo and redo changes
Expected Behavior: Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Shift+Z restore placement deletion and transforms without stale references

User Story: As an author, I can enable grid snapping
Expected Behavior: Translation rotation and scale snap to configured finite increments when enabled

User Story: As an author, I can hide and lock layers
Expected Behavior: Visibility toggles affect the intended systems and locked layers cannot be selected; view state is not serialized

User Story: As an author, my edits autosave
Expected Behavior: Dirty changes save after debounce; reload resets pending stale saves; status reports success or error

User Story: As an author, I can configure collider proxies
Expected Behavior: Collider types update support/blocking behavior and optional debug wireframes without depending on visual mesh complexity

User Story: As an author, I can import GLB assets
Expected Behavior: Valid GLB/GLTF imports enter the asset library with budget and animation metadata; invalid/oversized input is reported

User Story: As an author, I can import an image or draw a relief
Expected Behavior: Image/drawing input generates a preview; Generate/Invert/Clear work; Add Asset makes it placeable

User Story: As an author, I can save and place prefabs
Expected Behavior: Selection becomes a reusable prefab; placing expands normal objects with stable prefab references

User Story: As an author, I can browse built-in and saved prefabs
Expected Behavior: Library shows metadata and supports placement/instancing without losing object editability

User Story: As an author, I can import and export mod packages
Expected Behavior: JSON/ZIP mods validate register list export and provide their worlds/assets/prefabs/kits

User Story: As an author, I can generate a city
Expected Behavior: Parameters and seed produce deterministic normal world objects grouped by generator instance

User Story: As an author, I can generate camps ruins and forests
Expected Behavior: Each generator emits bounded deterministic layouts from selected prefab sources

User Story: As an author, I can generate roads plazas and connectors
Expected Behavior: Connective generators join authored origins and emit readable anchors/layout data

User Story: As a player, I can traverse a generated settlement
Expected Behavior: Connected procedural content loads visibly and forms a readable traversable runtime layout

User Story: As an author, I can create splines masks and modifiers
Expected Behavior: Editable authoring primitives serialize; derived beacon trail updates; undo/redo restores changes

User Story: As an author/player, imported animations preview and play
Expected Behavior: Rigged asset clips preview in editor and independent instances animate correctly at runtime

User Story: As an author, I can configure triggers doors pickups signs and spawns
Expected Behavior: Role-specific fields serialize and runtime enter/exit/open/close/collect/teleport behaviors fire through channels

User Story: As an author, I can configure particle emitters
Expected Behavior: Emitters preview while editor is open and run with validated settings in play mode

User Story: As an author, I can edit lighting and fog
Expected Behavior: Panel changes apply live and round-trip to runtime with sanitized colors/intensities/distances

User Story: As an author, I can voxelize a selected object for debugging
Expected Behavior: Voxel occupancy is deterministic bounded and rendered as one instanced mesh; clear removes preview

User Story: As a player, terrain rendering and collision agree
Expected Behavior: Mesh sampling placement and player grounding share one profile-backed height source

User Story: As an author, I can use rolling or alpine terrain
Expected Behavior: Profiles are deterministic finite and visually/geometrically distinct with valid spawn/placement fields

User Story: As a player, terrain material renders correctly
Expected Behavior: Macro detail slope rock height tint and detail settings compile and render without GPU errors

User Story: As a player, grass streams with authored appearance
Expected Behavior: Grass placement is grounded; clumping/tint/Fresnel compile; visible patches and draw calls stay bounded

User Story: As a player, trees stream around the camera
Expected Behavior: Instanced tree patches honor terrain/exclusions/LOD and rebuild after editor changes

User Story: As a player, bushes stream with authored settings
Expected Behavior: Bushes honor enabled/density/seed/clumping and remain within bounded patch draw calls

User Story: As a player, glacial water follows terrain masks
Expected Behavior: Alpine water is deterministic and above masked ground; rolling profile has no unintended water

User Story: As a player, valley atmosphere is coherent
Expected Behavior: Fog/sky/lighting configuration validates and produces finite profile-appropriate values

User Story: As a player, the alpine valley renders as intended
Expected Behavior: Terrain water atmosphere grass lighting and grounded player render together without console errors

User Story: As a player, grounded wildlife inhabits legal terrain
Expected Behavior: Deterministic animals stay habitat-clamped grounded dry bounded and instanced

User Story: As a player, bird flocks inhabit the sky
Expected Behavior: Deterministic snow finches remain above terrain/water in bounded instanced flocks

User Story: As a player, ambient motes appear in legal habitat
Expected Behavior: Fireflies stream as instanced motes above terrain/water and within configured altitude bounds

User Story: As a player, world regions stream predictably
Expected Behavior: Region activation retention and disposal are deterministic and bounded around the camera

User Story: As a player, distant work sleeps without visual popping
Expected Behavior: Visibility kernel assigns tiers with guard bands and preserves visible/shadow meshes during fast turns

User Story: As a player, repeated static content batches efficiently
Expected Behavior: Runtime batches compatible primitives while editor identity selection and collision remain intact

User Story: As a player, large procedural geometry pages safely
Expected Behavior: Paged producer builds/evicts deterministic chunks under budgets and rejects stale async results

User Story: As a player, distant/near geometry has stable depth
Expected Behavior: Reverse-Z capability is reported honestly and the precision proof renders correct occlusion

User Story: As an author, I can generate procedural weapons
Expected Behavior: Lab renders a weapon; seed/type/sliders/presets reroll valid non-empty recipes; orbit and zoom work

User Story: As an author, generated weapons retain identity
Expected Behavior: Recipe/descriptor identity is deterministic and survives placement serialization/reload

User Story: As an author, I can place generated weapons in a world
Expected Behavior: Armed weapon click creates a grounded runtime weapon with finite markers and persisted descriptor

User Story: As a player, I can equip drop and store a weapon
Expected Behavior: Nearby weapon equips to the player at a finite hand marker; drop restores world placement; store hides safely

User Story: As a player, I can select hand back and hip slots
Expected Behavior: Keys 1/2/3 and cycling attach with correct finite orientation and enforce one occupant per slot

User Story: As a player, I can carry and manage multiple weapons
Expected Behavior: F picks up/draws; R cycles; H holsters/draws; G drops active; carried weapons persist according to policy

User Story: As a player, I can attack with an active weapon
Expected Behavior: Left click follows the combat path; valid target takes bounded damage with hit feedback; invalid/occluded targets do not

User Story: As a player, I can fight a glacial sentinel
Expected Behavior: Enemy validates loads reacts to hits transitions states and persists defeat without non-finite state

User Story: As a player, living enemies patrol authored bounds
Expected Behavior: Patrol follows authored points terrain-safely within encounter radius and stops after defeat

User Story: As a player, I encounter distinct sentinel and frost-wisp archetypes
Expected Behavior: Both share valid combat/FSM behavior while data drives distinct health silhouette feedback and patrol traits

User Story: As an author, I can place and remove combat encounters
Expected Behavior: Editor writes validated descriptors and preview rings without spawning editor enemies

User Story: As a player, authored encounters project and complete
Expected Behavior: Entering/playing a beat creates one enemy; defeat completes/persists per replay policy and presentation updates

User Story: As a player, combat beats are readable
Expected Behavior: Idle-to-alert telegraph zone/gate cues banner and clear feedback track encounter state without mutating it

User Story: As a player, I can complete the relic weapon objective
Expected Behavior: Relic/cache spawn safely; prompt/equip/carry/open sequence advances; completion persists and can restart

User Story: As a new player, I can complete the integrated loop
Expected Behavior: Living world loads; guidance teaches movement; weapon objective and encounter can be completed end to end

User Story: As a new player, I receive timely prompts without editor leakage
Expected Behavior: Play mode shows compact arrival/objective/interaction/completion guidance; movement dismisses teaching; L toggles trace

User Story: As a player, hostile saved/input data cannot break the session
Expected Behavior: Spawn resolves dry/grounded; non-finite markers/dt are rejected; reload remains deterministic

User Story: As a player, I can play and replay the frozen-cache slice
Expected Behavior: Beats/prompts/landmarks/completion card advance in order; keep exploring and play again behave distinctly

User Story: As a player, I can complete repeated combat beats
Expected Behavior: Benchmark stages multiple authored encounters with independent completion and stable reload behavior

User Story: As an exploring player, I can discover optional content
Expected Behavior: Off-route shrine provides discovery/pickup/reward feedback while the main slice remains completable

User Story: As a player, key milestones have audio and visual cues
Expected Behavior: Shared audio emits differentiated one-shot cues and accessible toast equivalents without duplicate wind engines

User Story: As a player, the benchmark corridor is intentional and playable
Expected Behavior: Relic overlook crossing encounters cache and optional content load visibly measurably and reload-safely

User Story: As a user, representative scenes stay within structural budgets
Expected Behavior: Performance contract classifies canonical scenes and live counts/draw calls remain within declared budgets

User Story: As an author, I can see budget health
Expected Behavior: Budget HUD displays current metrics and classification in editor/runtime without changing gameplay

User Story: As a developer, I can inspect WebGPU feasibility honestly
Expected Behavior: Isolated lab reports supported/unsupported/error capability without pretending the production renderer migrated

User Story: As a maintainer, I can build all app entry points
Expected Behavior: Vite production build emits editor Arsenal Lab and WebGPU Lab without unresolved imports

User Story: As a maintainer, I can run repository skill/layout/smoke gates
Expected Behavior: Three.js adoption layout production build and browser smoke gates complete without errors
